So, when somebody gives you money, so it's like, if you say, now I've gotten very lazy about this, but if you sell from the front of the room to a group, you will always sell more when you got the stuff at the back of the room for them to walk out the door with than if you're going to ship it to them later. Now again, I've gotten very lazy, so I'm a poor example in recent years because I don't care. I'm not really sure what they're doing at all because I don't care, but it's neither here or there. And if you're not going to have all the stuff there, you want to at least have something. So when they walk out the door, they got something because otherwise they're giving you money for nothing and nobody likes that, right? So what's the value of any kind of opportunity? It's delayed, right? There's no value improvement, repair or opportunity. There's no real value until they go use the stuff and get a result, right? Two problems with that. Delay, and the fact that most of them are never going to use anything and get any result. So we want to find ways to give them value by their perception, so it doesn't matter if it's real or not. But by their perception, value that does not require activity or result. So there's a link thing, status, which we'll come to. But so the Brainerd-Mellinger story. So I want to tell you the Brainerd story. And then we're probably, unfortunately, going to be out of time. So Brainerd, for many years, he's in the Opportunity magazines. He's dead now. He was called the International Traders Association. And the pitch really was a course on import and export plus access to a bunch of wholesale sources for product. George, are you up there on the platform? Yeah. Do you ever meet Oman-Mellinger? No. See, you would like Brainerd. It's funny. Funny as she is. Weird as you are, in his own way. So International Traders basically sold a home study course that they didn't call it that and access to a bunch of wholesale merchandise that you could then sell if you didn't want to use the home study course and actually do import and export. They brought it in for you and you could get it. So and I don't remember price points anymore. It's irrelevant. So here's what Brainerd tells me. He says, you want to hear about a breakthrough in our business? You want to hear about a real game changer, something that really changed this thing? I said, sure. I'd love to hear about it. He said, so we got this big refund rate, right? We shipped these courses and everybody now realizes they got to do a bunch of work and there's 12 manuals and so it comes back as fast as we ship it out the door. He says, no good. He says, so we're trying to figure this out. We're trying to figure this out and we're trying to figure this out and we can't figure it out. He said, we're making money, but still. So he says, one day I get this idea. Now really, what do they want? What is this really all about? He said, really, they want to be in the import-export business, but it's not so much about being in a business. It's about thinking they're in the business and being able to say they're in the business. Because really what they want to do is be more important to the people around them. So he said, what does everybody do when they get in a real business? Bingo. I didn't answer it right away, but you did. For instance, what does everybody do when they get in a real business? Acknowledging, of course, that his was not a real business. I said, I don't know. He says, they all run out and get business cards. That's what they do. The first thing they do is they get business cards. He said, so here's what we do now. As soon as we get the order, we print them up 500 business cards, international traders association, import-export consultant with their name and their address and their phone number on it, and we ship them the business cards even before we ship them the course. We send the course like two days after we ship the business cards. He says, guess what's happened to our refund rate? Drop like a rock. He said, so really now we're in a business card business. That's a business word. We're in a business card business. He said, the other stuff is superfluous. There's magic in what he did. He really recognized that people want to be a part of something. Why do they want to be a part of something? Because it links to whatever on your list, number 15, they want elevation of status. So let's skip over to 15. So let's go to page 232. So here's an ad. This is on the cover of your manual too. So 233 has an ad from international correspondent schools, which was a client of mine for a number of years, but this goes all the way back to 1919. I don't go back that far. But so here's an ad from international correspondent schools from 1919. And the guy is handing grace some spending money here, and boy, here's an extra 50 bucks I'm making real money now. You flip it over to 234. This adds from the 1990s all the way into the early 2000. This is the same damn ad. It's 56,000. She's looking at a checkbook. Instead of being handed cash, same ad, same appeal. 235 is from 2005. You can't really see the photograph. Doesn't make any difference. You can read for Tracy, for Tracy. I was finally able to purchase the kind of autumn that she deserves. You flip over to 236 also from 2005. Again you can't really see the picture at top, but they're on a tennis court and she's kissing them. In 237, she's here, this is from 2010. She's looking over her shoulder at the laptop adoringly because he's got his own business website and he's making money. So you go back to status. So the real reason why, and this is gender specific, Ally, so I don't have any answer for you, but the real reason why men buy opportunity is to get laid. That's why men buy opportunity. Same reason they take out the trash. And by the way, the empirical evidence that it does not work does not discourage them in the least. So here's the bigger thing to get about this. Often they're not buying the thing for the practical reason that we think they are buying it. Often they are not buying it for their personal goals. In fact, hardly anybody ever sticks to their own personal goals. They're buying it essentially to try and fulfill the expectations of other people and elevate their status in the eyes of others.
